revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:榛名神社 (富士見市)]]
]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Haruna Shrine (榛名神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujimi, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Fujimi, Saitama|en|Fujimi, Saitama|qid=Q495809|lt=Fujimi, Saitama|1=Fujimi, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Katsuse, Fujimi|ja|勝瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuse, Fujimi|1=katsuse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16278969}} 791 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 51 | Latitude_sec = 56.3 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 32 | Longitude_sec = 27.7 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|埴上姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Other Names = Katsuse Haruna Shrine (勝瀬榛名神社) | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Fujimi|qid=Q495809|1=Fujimi, Saitama}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.865639|N|139.541028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Haruna Shrine''' (榛名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujimi, Saitama|en|Fujimi, Saitama|qid=Q495809|lt=Fujimi, Saitama|1=Fujimi, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|qid=Q877831|lt=Maya, mother of the Buddha|1=Maya (mother of the Buddha)}}, who had arrived in {{ill|Itsukushima|en|Itsukushima|qid=Q875301|lt=Itsukushima|1=Itsukushima}} during the 25th year of Emperor Kōshō’s reign, was later enshrined at this location. Additionally, a ridge plaque from "Bunmei Ninth Year, Cyclical Year Hinototori (1477)" records a reconstruction, indicating that the shrine had already existed by that time<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, North Saitama, Chichibu'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1986, pp. 500–501.</ref>.
In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was classified as a "village shrine." In {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), due to the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|qid=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, four nearby shrines were merged into it<ref name="Shrine"></ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Ginkgo Tree''' (Designated as a Natural Monument by Fujimi City, June 20, 1983)<ref name="City">[https://www.city.fujimi.saitama.jp/miru_tanoshimu/syougaigaku/bunkazai/2010-0423-1925-137/shiteibunkazai.files/ichouhp.pdf Ginkgo Tree] - Fujimi City</ref>
== Access == * 26-minute walk from {{ill|Fujimino Station|en|Fujimino Station|qid=Q5507442|lt=Fujimino Station|1=Fujimino Station}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Iruma, North Saitama, Chichibu'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1986.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}
